title: Build without Volume Mount
body:
as it's currently not working for me and maybe for others.
But I don't know about other people's opinion, I find building this way
even more elegent as the build tooling writer has more
control over what to actually take.

```
$ python -c "import this"
The Zen of Python, by Tim Peters

Beautiful is better than ugly.
Explicit is better than implicit. <- i.e. it's this
Simple is better than complex.
[...]
```

I'll still continue debugging the volume mounting though, no worries.

comments.body:
This does require that we rebuild the image every time, though.

I tend to think we should go the other way - not add the source to the image, but instead exclusively mount it in.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhdojun* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **vrothberg** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @vrothberg` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Did some debugging, aaand:

> This does require that we rebuild the image every time, though.

Local build artifacts [should be in the build image](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7218/commits/0202eabe06f293eccef5dfb9e93322f7d443c765#diff-3254677a7917c6c01f55212f86c57fbfR12) the next round as well.

> I tend to think we should go the other way - not add the source to the image, but instead exclusively mount it in.

That might be an option as well. This doesn't come without disadvantages though, as of course it potentially modifies the local filesystem as well in undesired ways if there's a bug in the tooling. (E.g. with my shell script I also locally deleted my podman completely at one step, which then also stopped me from building a new podman.)
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon @rhdojun, ping :) I let you decide about a direction.

I personally understand that both directions have their pros and cons but felt much more comfortable with the copy step when using podman as build tool.

Also looked around a little what other parties/solutions would do, and found that [Docker's multi-stage builds](https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/multistage-build/) go a similar path:

```
FROM buildenv:latest
//build app

FROM deployenv:latest
COPY --from=0 /go/src/github.com/alexellis/href-counter/app     <- here
//use app
```
@mheon @rhdojun We need a final decision on this.  @mheon if you want to open this for discussion at meetup next week, we can do it then.

We completely removed the endpoint, AFAIK, so this is likely no longer
relevant.
